title: Kailasanathar Temple, Kanchipuram
tagline: Kailasanathar Temple, Kanchipuram — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: The Kailasanathar Temple, Kanchipuram, also referred to as the Kailasanatha temple, is a Pallava-era historic Hindu temple in Kanchipuram, Tamil Nadu, India.
overview.description: The Kailasanathar Temple, Kanchipuram, also referred to as the Kailasanatha temple, is a Pallava-era historic Hindu temple in Kanchipuram, Tamil Nadu, India. Dedicated to Shiva, it is one of the oldest surviving monuments in Kanchipuram. It reflects a Dravidian architecture and was built around 700 CE by Narasimhavarman II with additions by Mahendravarman III. A square-plan temple, it has a mukha-mandapa (entrance hall), a maha-mandapa (gathering hall) and a primary garbha-griya (sanctum) topped with a four-storey vimana. The main sanctum is surrounded by nine shrines, seven outside and two in

topPlaces.name: Anekadhangavadeswarar temple
topPlaces.description: Anekadhangavadeswarar Temple (also called Anegathangavadham) is a Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva, located in the town of Kanchipuram, near Kailasanathar temple, Kanchipuram district in Tamil Nadu, India. Anekadhangavadeswarar is revered in the 7th century Tamil Saiva canonical work, the Tevaram, written by Tamil saint poets known as the nayanars and classified as Paadal Petra Sthalam, the 275 temples revered in the canon.

topPlaces.name: Pandava Thoothar Perumal Temple
topPlaces.description: Pandavatutar Perumal Temple or Thirupadagam located in Kanchipuram in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, is dedicated to the Hindu god Krishna, an avatar of the god Vishnu. Constructed in the Dravidian style of architecture, the temple is extolled in the Nalayira Divya Prabandham, the early medieval Tamil canon of the Alvar saints from the 6th–9th centuries CE. It is one of the 108 Divya Desams dedicated to Vishnu, who is worshipped as Pandava Tutar Perumal (Krishna) and his consort Lakshmi (Rukmini).

topPlaces.name: Metraleeswar temple
topPlaces.description: Metraleeswarar Temple (also called Kanchi Metrali) is a Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva, located in Pillaiyar Palayam area in Kanchipuram, Kanchipuram district in Tamil Nadu, India. Metraleeswarar is revered in the 7th-century CE Tamil Saiva canonical work, the Tevaram, written by Tamil saint poets known as the nayanars and classified as Paadal Petra Sthalam, the 275 temples revered in the canon.

topPlaces.name: Ona Kantheeswarar Temple
topPlaces.description: Ona Kantheeswarar Temple (also called Onakanthali) is a Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva, located in the town of Panjupettai, Kanchipuram, Kanchipuram district in Tamil Nadu, India. Shiva is worshipped as Ona Kantheeswarar and his consort Parvathi as Kamakshi.

topPlaces.name: Kanchi Kamakoti Peetham
topPlaces.description: Sri Kanchi Kamakoti Peetham, also called the Sri Kanchi Matham or the Moolamnaya Sarvagnya Peetham, is a Hindu religious center of Vedic learning, located in Kanchipuram, Tamil Nadu. It is located near the Kamakshi Amman Temple of the Shaktism tradition, which also contains a shrine dedicated to the alleged final resting place (disputed) of the Advaita Vedanta teacher Adi Shankara.

topPlaces.name: Nilathingal Thundam Perumal temple
topPlaces.description: Nilathingal Thundam Perumal temple (also called Chandrasoodeswarar Perumal temple) is a Hindu temple dedicated to Vishnu, located in Kanchipuram in the state of Tamil Nadu, India. The temple is located in a shrine in Ekambareswarar Temple, the largest temple in the town of Kanchipuram, located in the northern part of the town.

topPlaces.name: Ekambareswarar Temple (Kanchipuram)
topPlaces.description: Ekambareswarar Temple (Kanchi Ekambaranathar Thirukkovil) is a Hindu temple dedicated to the god Shiva, located in the town of Kanchipuram in Tamil Nadu, India. It is significant to the Hindu sect of Saivism as one of the temples associated with the five elements, the Pancha Bhoota Stalas, and specifically the element of earth, or Prithvi.

topPlaces.name: Karchapeswarar Temple
topPlaces.description: Karchapeswarar Temple is a Hindu temple located in the town of Kanchipuram in Tamil Nadu, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kailasanathar Temple, Kanchipuram?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kailasanathar Temple, Kanchipuram is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kailasanathar Temple, Kanchipuram?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chennai International Airport (55 km) and the nearest railway station is Chennai Central (69 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kailasanathar Temple, Kanchipuram?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kailasanathar Temple, Kanchipuram at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kailasanathar Temple, Kanchipuram?
faq.a: Stays in Kailasanathar Temple, Kanchipuram start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kailasanathar Temple, Kanchipuram?
faq.a: The top attractions are Anekadhangavadeswarar temple, Pandava Thoothar Perumal Temple, Metraleeswar temple, Ona Kantheeswarar Temple, Kanchi Kamakoti Peetham.
